provenance.derivation: Option B (P0-21, user sign-off 2026-07-10): per-element extents are transcribed VERBATIM from tboxevo crystal-derived files. No functional-element boundary is invented (CLAUDE.md §10.3). Where a deposition does not resolve an element to a residue extent, that element is ABSENT (omitted), not guessed.
source_dirs: data/external/secondarystructure/<ID>/<ID>-2D.dbn (DSSR crystal-derived dot-bracket of the leader strand)
provenance.coordinate_frame: 1-based inclusive, leader-strand local frame (matches src/tbox_finder/labels.py ELEMENT_COORDS). apical_segment_slice values in the source JSON are 0-indexed inclusive; converted here to 1-based inclusive (a,b) -> (a+1,b+1).

class_assignment.note: T-box class is not invented: it follows the tboxevo _curate_note descriptor ('ultrashort-translational' = class II; 'classical'/'Stem I domain' aminoacyl-tRNA-synthetase leader = class I) corroborated by the in-repo literature already ≥2-source verified (P0-17 leakage-test evidence chain).

entries.name: 4LCK
source_files: cocrystal_ss_cons/anchor_4LCK.json (_status=filled; seq_len=102; leader_strand=strand_C; 'Stem I domain'; apical_segment_slice [38,67])

entries.name: 2KZL
source_files: cocrystal_ss_cons/anchor_2KZL.json (_status=filled; NMR; seq_len=55; leader_strand=strand_A; 'Specifier domain + GA motif region' of Stem I; apical_segment_slice [21,36])

entries.name: 6POM
entries.note: Full-length cryo-EM (Zhang 2019). tboxevo records only the Stem-I apical slice; the Stem-I 3' boundary and the downstream Stem-II/antiterminator residue extents are NOT recorded, so NO functional extent is asserted (all-background painting). Contributes only an in-frame apical-diagnostic consistency check.

entries.name: WCBNVLFN
entries.note: The recorded apical slice [9,96] overruns the recorded Stem_I extent (1,95) by 1 nt (the apical slice carries a ±5-nt pad convention). Recorded faithfully as an observed cross-source boundary-noise instance; the label assertion uses the Stem_I extent, and the apical consistency check tolerates the pad overhang for this entry.

entries.name: 6UFG
entries.note: Actinobacteria ileS ultrashort-translational (class II). tboxevo anchor is a scaffold: the _curate_note gives only an APPROXIMATE prose antiterminator-helix position (~78-81), not a residue extent, so NO functional extent is asserted (CLAUDE.md §10.3). Validates the class-II routing + naive-withholding on a real class-II crystal.
source_files: cocrystal_ss_cons/anchor_6UFG.json (scaffold; 'Mycobacterium tuberculosis IleS T-box leader'; ss_cons len 166)

entries.name: 6UFH
entries.note: Actinobacteria ileS ultrashort-translational (class II), alternate state. Prose-only T-loop position (~38-50), not a residue extent, so NO functional extent asserted. Validates class-II routing + naive-withholding on a real class-II crystal.
source_files: cocrystal_ss_cons/anchor_6UFH.json (scaffold; 'Mycobacterium tuberculosis IleS T-box leader'; ss_cons len 167)

entries.name: 6UFM
entries.note: Actinobacteria ileS ultrashort-translational (class II). The Stem-II extent (30,77) is transcribed from the mapped span of stem2_anchor_to_aln_col (0-indexed [29,76]). This is the ONLY class-II deposition with a residue-resolved element. It is the load-bearing case for the naive-withholds-TBDB001.cm assertion: the production label paints Stem_II, but the class-II-CM-naive label withholds it (all background).
source_files: cocrystal_ss_cons/anchor_6UFM.json ('Stem II + ultrashort Stem I'; stem2_anchor_to_aln_col mapped span 0-idx [29,76] -> 1-based [30,77]; 48 nt)

label_noise_ceiling.adr_ref: ADR-0004 D6 (the 9-PDB cross-source label-noise ceiling C, reported non-gated; governs delta-recalibration of the 0.80 GATE-4 floor)
label_noise_ceiling.finding: 5 depositions resolve Stem-I extents; 6POM is a full-length deposition with no asserted functional extent, and the 3 class-II ileS leaders carry no residue-resolved elements beyond 6UFM's Stem-II, so NONE of the 9 depositions resolves Specifier / Antiterminator / Terminator / Discriminator to a residue extent. A numeric per-nucleotide cross-source F1 ceiling over the full 8-class target is therefore NOT estimable from these depositions alone (and a within-fixture agreement would be circular, since the fixture extents ARE the crystal source). One cross-source boundary-noise instance is observed (WCBNVLFN apical/Stem_I ±pad overhang of 1 nt).
label_noise_ceiling.delta_governance_conclusion: C does not demonstrate that the cross-source annotation caps achievable per-nt F1 below 0.80 (it is not estimable here and shows no sub-floor cap on the resolvable classes), so per ADR-0004 D6 the GATE-4 floor STAYS 0.80 (the N<=9 crystal ceiling must not one-directionally lower the bar). A rigorous numeric C would require the full literature-grounded per-element annotation (the deferred Option-A path) aligned to matched corpus records.
